Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Design, setting, and participants' section of the Abstract, it states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'. On page 2, in the 'Patients and methods' section, it further confirms: 'From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm'.

Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 2, under '2. Patients and methods', it states: 'From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.' The ADT arm is the control arm. This is confirmed on page 3 under '3. Results' and in Table 1 'Characteristics of the patients (n = 385)' where 'ADT (n = 193)' is listed.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 1, in the 'Results and limitations' section of the Abstract, it states: 'After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo'. On page 3, in section '3.1. Overall survival', it states: 'After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo (95% CI, 82.9-84.7)'. On page 4, in 'Table 2 - Survival data in the GETUG-AFU 15 and CHAARTED studies by treatment arm and metastatic burden', the row 'Median follow-up, mo' for 'GETUG-AFU 15' shows '83.9'.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 2, in the 'Patients and methods' section, it states: 'From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.' The 'ADT plus D arm' is the experimental arm.

Treatment Arm(s).value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel; ADT Alone
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The title of the paper states: "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer". This is further confirmed in the 'Design, setting, and participants' section of the Abstract: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients" and in the 'Patients and methods' section: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone.".

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'Design, setting, and participants' section in the abstract on page 1 states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'. This indicates two arms. Further, on page 2, under '2. Patients and methods', it is explicitly stated: '385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm. Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (...) or ADT alone.' This confirms two arms.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The title of the paper is "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone...". The 'Design, setting, and participants' section states: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients". In '2. Patients and methods', it further clarifies: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone." Treatment Arm 1 is ADT plus Docetaxel. ADT (Androgen Deprivation Therapy) is a hormonal therapy, and Docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent. Therefore, the class of agents is Hormonal therapy and Chemotherapy.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles). ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors.
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: Found in the 'Design, setting, and participants' section on page 1 and further detailed in '2. Patients and methods' on page 2, stating that patients were randomized to 'ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles)' and describing the ADT component.

Control Arm.evidence: The title of the paper states "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone". The abstract under "Design, setting, and participants" mentions "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone". In the 'Patients and methods' section on page 2, it states "385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm. Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (...) or ADT alone." Table 1 and figures consistently refer to the control group as "ADT" or "ADT arm".

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document, which reports on the GETUG-AFU15 trial, does not mention or report any results related to the quality of life of participants. While a reference [26] mentions 'quality of life' in the context of a different trial (GETUG 12), it is not for the trial discussed in this paper.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document, which reports on the GETUG-AFU15 trial, focuses on survival outcomes (overall survival, biochemical progression-free survival, radiographic progression-free survival) and does not mention any assessment of participants' overall well-being, satisfaction with health status, or a 'Quality of Life Scale' for the participants of this specific trial. While a reference [26] mentions 'quality of life' in the context of a different trial (GETUG 12 trial), it is not reported for the GETUG-AFU15 trial in this document.

NCT.evidence: Found on page 3, under '3. Results', stating 'The trial is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT00104715).'

PubMed ID.evidence: The PubMed ID for this specific publication is not explicitly reported in the document.

Trial Name.evidence: Found in the title of the article: 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer: Impact of Metastatic Burden and Long-term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial'. The trial is consistently referred to as GETUG-AFU15.

Author.evidence: Found on page 1, in the author list: 'Gwenaelle Gravis et al.'

Year.evidence: Found on page 1, top right corner: 'EUROPEAN UROLOGY 70 (2016)256-262'.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is a multi-page article with detailed sections including Introduction, Patients and methods, Results, Discussion, and References, indicating a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: Found in the title of the article: 'Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial'. Also mentioned in the abstract: 'Randomized phase 3 trial'.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The title includes 'Long-term Survival Analysis' and the abstract states 'Objective: To assess the impact of metastatic burden and to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study.', indicating a follow-up analysis of an existing trial.

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly stated in the title: 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer: Impact of Metastatic Burden and Long-term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial'. It is also referred to as 'GETUG-AFU15 study' in the abstract and throughout the document, for example, in the 'Objective' section of the abstract and in the 'Introduction' section on page 2: 'In the phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 study, we assessed the benefits...' and in the 'Results' section on page 3: 'The GETUG-AFU15 trial randomized 385 patients...'.

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'Abstract' section, under 'Outcome measurements and statistical analysis', it states: 'Primary end point was OS.' Additionally, in section '2.1. Statistics' on page 2, it confirms: 'The primary end point was OS.'

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progres-sion-free survival (rPFS)
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: The 'Abstract' section, under 'Outcome measurements and statistical analysis', explicitly states: 'Primary end point was OS. Secondary end points were biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progres-sion-free survival (rPFS).' This is reiterated in section '2.1. Statistics'.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: The 'Patients and methods' section on page 2 states that '385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm. Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone.' This indicates that all 192 patients in the ADT plus D (treatment) arm received docetaxel during the trial.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: The 'Patients and methods' section on page 2 states that 'Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone.' This indicates that patients in the ADT alone (control) arm did not receive docetaxel as part of their assigned treatment during the trial. The text on page 5 mentions 'In the ADT arm, 127 of 149 patients (85%) treated for progressive disease received D', but this refers to subsequent treatment after progression, not administration during the initial trial period.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Gleason score, n (%)' for '<7' row and 'ADT plus D (n = 192)' column.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Gleason score, n (%)' for '<7' row and 'ADT (n = 193)' column.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≥ 8 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Gleason score, n (%)' for '>8' row and 'ADT plus D (n = 192)' column.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≥ 8 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Gleason score, n (%)' for '>8' row and 'ADT (n = 193)' column.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: On page 3, in the 'Results' section, the document states: 'Patients with metastatic disease after failure of local treatment (28%) had a significantly longer median OS than those with metastases at diagnosis (72%) at 83.1 mo (95% CI, 68.6-NR) versus 46.5 mo (95% CI, 40.5-54.6)'. 'Metastases at diagnosis' refers to synchronous metastases. This indicates that results were reported based on this prognostic group.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: On page 3, in the 'Results' section, the document states: 'Patients with metastatic disease after failure of local treatment (28%) had a significantly longer median OS than those with metastases at diagnosis (72%) at 83.1 mo (95% CI, 68.6-NR) versus 46.5 mo (95% CI, 40.5-54.6)'. 'Metastatic disease after failure of local treatment' refers to metachronous diseases. This indicates that results were reported based on this prognostic group.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: On page 2, in the 'Introduction' section, the document states: 'We present long-term outcomes in the overall population and in the HVD and LVD subgroups.' HVD stands for High-Volume Disease. On page 2, in the 'Results' section, it further reports: 'In patients with HVD, median OS was 4.7 mo longer in the ADT plus D arm...'. Table 2 on page 3 also provides 'HVD' data. This confirms reporting by high volume prognostic groups.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: On page 2, in the 'Introduction' section, the document states: 'We present long-term outcomes in the overall population and in the HVD and LVD subgroups.' LVD stands for Low-Volume Disease. On page 2, in the 'Results' section, it further reports: 'In patients with LVD, median OS was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5-NR) in the ADT plus D arm...'. Table 2 on page 3 also provides 'LVD' data. This confirms reporting by low volume prognostic groups.

Treatment Class.evidence: The title of the paper is 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer'. Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) is a form of hormonal therapy, and Docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent. The introduction also discusses these treatments in the context of prostate cancer.

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) plus Docetaxel (D) versus ADT alone. ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors. Docetaxel was administered at 75 mg/m² for 21 days, up to nine cycles.
Type of Therapy.evidence: The title of the paper is 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer'. The 'Design, setting, and participants' section of the Abstract states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'. In the 'Patients and methods' section, it further specifies: 'Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone. ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors.' This information describes the type of treatment provided.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a research paper presenting the results and limitations of a specific randomized controlled trial (GETUG-AFU15). It discusses limitations of its own study, such as retrospective analysis and lack of statistical power for subgroup analysis, which affect the strength of its evidence. However, it does not provide an 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT)' as a general assessment or using a standardized framework for evaluating the quality of evidence across RCTs.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The document reports median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) in the ADT plus D arm as 22.9 mo (95% CI, 19.5-28.4) and median radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) in the ADT plus D arm as 22.9 mo (95% CI, 20.5-31.4) for the overall population (page 4, section 3.2). The hazard ratio for bPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) is 0.67 (95% CI, 0.54–0.84; p < 0.001) and for rPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) is 0.69 (95% CI, 0.55–0.87; p = 0.002). A PFS rate in percentage is not reported.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The document reports median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) in the ADT arm as 12.9 mo (95% CI, 11.9–17.7) and median radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) in the ADT arm as 15.3 mo (95% CI, 12.4-19.8) for the overall population (page 4, section 3.2). The hazard ratio for bPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) is 0.67 (95% CI, 0.54–0.84; p < 0.001) and for rPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) is 0.69 (95% CI, 0.55–0.87; p = 0.002). A PFS rate in percentage is not reported.
